$comment: Neutral shipped <threads.h> (C11 threads) — FC17.9(a), COMPLETE (Cycle 1 the NON-trampoline slice + Cycle 2 D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TRAMPOLINES: thrd_create/call_once/thrd_join). TWO-PART design: on elf the C11 thread API is a DIRECT libc FFI (glibc>=2.34 exports thrd_*/mtx_*/cnd_*/tss_* from libc.so.6 directly — verified: readelf shows `mtx_init@@GLIBC_2.34`, and a real gcc link of mtx_init WITHOUT -lpthread succeeds; the same libc.so.6 pthread.json already binds on the elf leg). On pe64 the CRT exports NO thrd_*, so each function is a COMPILER-SYNTHESIZED Win32 shim (Vehicle A): the `synthesize` tag routes the symbol to `src/mir/merge/synth_threads_shim.cpp`, which emits a tiny MIR body over the real kernel32 primitives (CRITICAL_SECTION / CONDITION_VARIABLE / Fls* / thread APIs — all in windows.json). ★ DUAL SAME-NAME ENTRIES per function (the errno __error/__errno_location split precedent): a pe entry (availableObjectFormats:["pe"] + `synthesize`) and an elf entry (["elf"], plain FFI, no tag). The `synthesize` VALUE equals the function NAME (a validated invariant — the loader rejects a mismatch or an unknown recipe id, closed vocabulary) so the pe64 synth pass identifies each recipe by its symbol name. ★ availableObjectFormats OMITS macho this cycle (macOS libSystem C11-threads export UNVERIFIED from a Windows host → D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO; #include <threads.h> on a macho target fails loud F_ShippedHeaderUnavailableForTarget). ★ __STDC_NO_THREADS__ is now macho-ONLY (Cycle 2): threads.h is COMPLETE on elf/pe64 (thrd_create landed → a conforming impl must NOT define the macro there, C11 6.10.8.3), so the c-subset.lang.json predefinedMacros row carries availableObjectFormats:["macho"] — the macro stays only on the carved-out macho leg (D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO). ★ Cycle 2 LANDED thrd_create (pe DIRECT-PASS to CreateThread — no closure/trampoline; the C11 int(*)(void*) start routine has the SAME x64 ABI as Win32 DWORD(*)(void*)), call_once (pe over InitOnceExecuteOnce via the module-scoped __dss_once_tramp adapter), and thrd_join (pe multi-block: WaitForSingleObject; if(res) GetExitCodeThread; CloseHandle — canonical StructCfMarkers rederived after finish()). STILL deferred on pe (elf FFI only, or omitted): thrd_equal, mtx_timedlock/cnd_timedwait AND thrd_sleep (D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TIMED — thrd_sleep's pe timespec read has an unverified time_t/long-width layout; a wrong offset is a silent miscompile). thrd_sleep therefore carries only an elf entry; the 20 pe single-block recipes + thrd_join (the 1 pe multi-block recipe) cover the rest. ★ TYPEDEF SIZES (per-format `variants`, mirroring pthread.json's opaque arr<i64,N>): thrd_t elf u64 (pthread_t) / pe ptr<void> (HANDLE). mtx_t elf arr<i64,6>=48 (pthread_mutex_t: 48 is arm64's __SIZEOF_PTHREAD_MUTEX_T MAX — over-allocates x86-64's 40 harmlessly, the pthread.json precedent, cross-arch-safe for the arm64 leg) / pe arr<i64,5>=40 (CRITICAL_SECTION on x64, = windows.json's CRITICAL_SECTION 8+4+4+8+8+8). cnd_t elf arr<i64,6>=48 (pthread_cond_t) / pe ptr<void>=8 (CONDITION_VARIABLE). once_flag elf i32 (pthread_once_t) / pe ptr<void>=8 (INIT_ONCE). tss_t u32 both (glibc pthread_key_t + Win FLS index=DWORD). thrd_start_t = ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->i32> (the real C11 int(*)(void*) — Cycle 2, so a conforming `thrd_create(&t,worker,&arg)` accepts a bare `worker` cast-free; a function→void* is NOT an implicit conversion in DSS, S0003, and thrd_create/call_once now type their callback params accurately so no source cast is needed; the pe synth still reads the callback as an opaque pointer, machine-uniform). tss_dtor_t ptr<void> (a Cycle-1 residual — only ever NULL in that witness; a NULL constant coerces to any pointer, so unaffected). thrd_t-by-VALUE fns (thrd_current/detach/join) diverge per format (elf u64 / pe ptr<void>) so their two entries carry DIFFERENT signatures — the dual-entry mechanism handles it. ★ CONSTANTS verified against glibc 2.39 /usr/include/threads.h (a real compile printed them): thrd_success=0/busy=1/error=2/nomem=3/timedout=4; mtx_plain=0/recursive=1/timed=2 — the SAME numeric values the pe recipes return, so a witness comparing ==thrd_success is portable.
